topics.label: Storage
topics.description: Persistent storage in Kubernetes: PersistentVolumes, PersistentVolumeClaims, StorageClasses, dynamic provisioning, volume access modes, and volume types including emptyDir, hostPath, and CSI drivers.

examScenarios.title: Provision persistent storage for a stateful application
examScenarios.prompt: Create a PersistentVolume of 1Gi with ReadWriteOnce access mode backed by a hostPath, then create a PVC that binds to it, and mount it into a pod at /data.
examScenarios.focus: PV/PVC lifecycle, binding rules, and pod volume mounts

examScenarios.title: Configure a StorageClass for dynamic provisioning
examScenarios.prompt: Create a StorageClass using the standard provisioner with a Retain reclaim policy, then create a PVC that uses it to dynamically provision a 500Mi volume.
examScenarios.focus: StorageClass parameters, reclaimPolicy, and PVC annotation

topics.label: Troubleshooting
topics.description: Diagnosing and resolving failures across nodes, pods, deployments, networking, and cluster components. Covers log access, event inspection, resource constraint debugging, and cluster component health checks.

examScenarios.title: Diagnose a pod stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
examScenarios.prompt: A pod in the 'prod' namespace keeps crashing. Use kubectl to inspect its logs, events, and describe output to determine the root cause and resolve it.
examScenarios.focus: kubectl logs, kubectl describe, exit codes, OOMKilled vs. config errors

examScenarios.title: Investigate a NotReady node
examScenarios.prompt: One node in the cluster shows NotReady status. Identify the failing component using kubectl and node-level systemd logs.
examScenarios.focus: kubelet status, journalctl, node conditions, cordoning

topics.label: Workloads & Scheduling
topics.description: Deploying and managing workloads using Deployments, ReplicaSets, StatefulSets, DaemonSets, Jobs, and CronJobs. Controlling pod placement through node selectors, affinity rules, taints, tolerations, and resource requests/limits.

studyPointers.label: Resource Management for Pods and Containers

examScenarios.title: Roll out a deployment with a canary strategy
examScenarios.prompt: Create a Deployment with 3 replicas, then perform a rolling update to a new image version using kubectl set image. Monitor rollout status and roll back if the new version fails.

examScenarios.title: Schedule a pod on a specific node using affinity
examScenarios.prompt: Label a node with 'disktype=ssd', then create a pod that uses node affinity to require scheduling only on nodes with that label.

topics.label: Cluster Architecture, Installation & Configuration
topics.description: Understanding Kubernetes control plane components, bootstrapping clusters with kubeadm, performing etcd backup and restore, configuring RBAC, managing kubeconfig, and working with CRDs and Helm.

examScenarios.title: Back up and restore etcd
examScenarios.prompt: Using etcdctl, create a snapshot of the etcd datastore, then restore from that snapshot to a new data directory and update the etcd static pod manifest to use it.
examScenarios.focus: etcdctl snapshot save/restore, ETCDCTL_API=3, static pod manifests

examScenarios.title: Create RBAC for a service account
examScenarios.prompt: Create a ServiceAccount 'app-reader' in the 'dev' namespace, a Role that allows get/list/watch on pods, and a RoleBinding that grants the role to the service account.

topics.label: Services & Networking
topics.description: Exposing applications through Services (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer), configuring Ingress resources, writing NetworkPolicies to control traffic, and understanding DNS resolution and CNI plugins.

examScenarios.title: Expose a deployment and restrict ingress traffic
examScenarios.prompt: Create a ClusterIP Service for a deployment, then write a NetworkPolicy that only allows pods with label 'role=frontend' to reach it on port 8080.
examScenarios.focus: Service selectors, NetworkPolicy podSelector and ports

examScenarios.title: Configure an Ingress resource
examScenarios.prompt: Create an Ingress that routes traffic for 'app.example.com/api' to a backend Service on port 80 using the nginx ingress class.
